pattern: With a tiny string you can enumerate every contiguous substring, keep the distinct prime values among them, and sum the three biggest.
hints: The string is short enough to look at every contiguous slice of it directly.
Turn each slice into an integer (which naturally drops leading zeros), test it for primality, and remember the distinct primes.
Collect all distinct primes you can form, sort them descending, and add up to the first three; if fewer exist, add them all.

edge_inputs.description: several distinct primes of varying length
leading zeros must be ignored when parsing
duplicate substrings count a prime once

stress.description: random short digit strings so primality and uniqueness both vary
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 10)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('0123456789') for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
stress.note: length capped at 10 by the constraints
